transitions.note: Peer review by Gemini 2.5 Pro (via PAL)
Add .ike/graph/ with YAML node files and four new tools (graph_create_node, graph_get_node, graph_ad

question: Should ike.md add a project-scoped knowledge graph (.ike/graph/) with YAML node files and typed edges, enabling AI agents to traverse connections between people, emails, artifacts, Wrike cards, and deadlines without re-discovering context each session?
context: Ike.md is a lightweight, file-based task execution system (MCP server, 21 tools). Projects are directories with .ike/ folders containing tasks, milestones, and documents. Currently projects are just containers — no attributes, no connections, no context about what they touch. The Director of AI cockpit needs agents to pick up work with full context across email (aic-mail MCP), Wrike (wrike MCP), research projects (research-md), GitHub repos, and people. Today these connections live in the human's head. The proposed solution adds a .ike/graph/ directory with YAML files (one per node), typed edges, and four new tools. Scale is ~200-500 nodes total. Must be git-friendly, LLM-readable, no external services. Validated by Gemini 2.5 Pro: graph inside ike (not separate service), flat projects with edge-based hierarchy (not recursive directories), YAML files correct at this scale, full graph better than single REFERENCES.yaml. PHART library can render ASCII graphs in terminal via NetworkX.
